mission.title: prometheus: Add mechanism to perform bulk imports
mission.description: Add mechanism to perform bulk imports. This issue affects 127+ users.

steps.title: Identify prometheus feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your prometheus deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=prometheus
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Add mechanism to perform bulk imports

steps.title: Check current resource configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant prometheus resources:
```bash
kubectl get resource -A
kubectl describe resource <name> -n <namespace>
```
Currently the only way to bulk-import data is a hacky one involving client-side timestamps and scrapes with multiple samples per time series. We should offer an API for bulk import. This relies on htt

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add mechanism to perform bulk imports
steps.description:
Presently metricSorter is unstable because Less returns "true" instead of "false" when two metrics compare the same.  This means when Collectors emit timestamped metrics in order, this sort will perturb the ordering and result in metrics delivered out-of-order to Prometheus.  This PR fixes the return value for Less.

Since we're going through the trouble of reordering metrics here anyway, explicitly sort by TimestampMs as well.

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Add mechanism to perform bulk imports is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=prometheus -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: Presently metricSorter is unstable because Less returns "true" instead of "false" when two metrics compare the same.  This means when Collectors emit timestamped metrics in order, this sort will perturb the ordering and result in metrics delivered out-of-order to Prometheus.  This PR fixes the return value for Less.

Since we're going through the trouble of reordering metrics here anyway, explicit. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with prometheus installed or the issue environment reproducible.
